---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-31"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# Section1.1-.3

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(readr)
library(class)
library(caret)
my_data <- read_csv("baseball.csv")
```

```{r}

library(GGally)
library(dplyr)

# Select a manageable subset of columns
df_small <- my_data %>% select(velocity, spin_rate, pitch_type,release_pos_z,release_pos_x)

# Run GGpairs
ggpairs(df_small)
```

# Sectoin 1.3 | Variable Explanation
The categorical variable we will be trying to predict is the type of pitch being 
thrown. There are 3 main categories with many different subcategories. Looking 
at the numeric variables, it seems that velocity and spring rate have the 
biggest impact, and release position on the x-axis also seems to have a largeish
impact. Velocity is measured in MPH, and spin rate is calculated with RPM. 
Release position X is the release position on the X axis, and release position 
Z is the release position on the Y axis. 

# Section 1.4 | Finding the Best K


```{r}
# Select relevant columns and drop NAs
baseball_knn <- my_data %>%
  select(velocity, spin_rate, release_pos_z, release_pos_x, pitch_type) %>%
  na.omit()

# Standardize numeric features (columns 1–4)
baseball_scaled <- baseball_knn
baseball_scaled[, 1:4] <- scale(baseball_scaled[, 1:4])
baseball_scaled <- data.frame(baseball_scaled)
baseball_scaled$pitch_type <- as.factor(baseball_scaled$pitch_type)

# Confirm standardization worked (means ~0, sd ~1)
sapply(baseball_scaled[, 1:4], mean)
sapply(baseball_scaled[, 1:4], sd)
```

```{r}
set.seed(239)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(baseball_scaled$pitch_type, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)

baseball_train <- baseball_scaled[trainIndex, ]
baseball_test  <- baseball_scaled[-trainIndex, ]

# Verify proportions are similar between sets
prop.table(table(baseball_train$pitch_type))
prop.table(table(baseball_test$pitch_type))
```

```{r}
set.seed(1)
error <- rep(0, 15)

for (i in 1:15) {
  knn_temp <- knn(train = baseball_train[, 1:4],
                  test  = baseball_test[, 1:4],
                  cl    = baseball_train$pitch_type,
                  k     = i)
  error[i] <- 1 - mean(knn_temp == baseball_test$pitch_type)
}

plot_data <- data.frame(k = 1:15, error)
ggplot(data = plot_data, aes(x = k, y = error)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue") +
  geom_point() +
  xlab("Neighborhood Size (k)") +
  ylab("Error Rate") +
  ggtitle("KNN Error Rate by k — Baseball Pitch Type")

best_k <- which.min(error)
cat("Best k:", best_k)
```


```{r}
set.seed(1)
knn_best <- knn(train = baseball_train[, 1:4],
                test  = baseball_test[, 1:4],
                cl    = baseball_train$pitch_type,
                k     = best_k)

# Confusion Matrix
cm <- table(Predicted = knn_best, Actual = baseball_test$pitch_type)
cm

# Overall accuracy
accuracy <- mean(knn_best == baseball_test$pitch_type)
cat("Accuracy:", round(accuracy, 4))

# Error rate
cat("Error Rate:", round(1 - accuracy, 4))
```

The model achieved 56.6% accuracy using k = 12. It classifies distinct pitch types well but struggles where velocity and spin rate overlap between classes, suggesting these four features alone have limits in separating every pitch type. 

#Section 1.5 | Classification Tree

```{r}
library(rpart)

classTree<- rpart(pitch_type ~ .,
                  data = baseball_train,
                  method = "class")

### PLOT TREE
library(rpart.plot)
rpart.plot(classTree)

### Plot CP
plotcp(classTree)

printcp(classTree)

## WHICH CP
minCP<-classTree$cptable[which.min(classTree$cptable[,"xerror"]),"CP"]
```
#### Step 4: Prune the Tree

Find the CP that minimizes the error.

```{r}
library(rpart.plot)

prune_classTree <- prune(classTree, cp = minCP )
rpart.plot(prune_classTree )
```
#### Step 5: Predict

```{r}
## DEFAULT TREE 
### PREDICT
predTree1<-predict(classTree , baseball_test, type="class")

### CONFUSION MATRIX
cmTree1<-table(baseball_test$pitch_type, predTree1)
cmTree1

#### CORRECT RATE
mean(baseball_test$pitch_type==predTree1)

## PRUNED TREE 
### PREDICT
predTree2<-predict(prune_classTree , baseball_test, type="class")

### CONFUSION MATRIX
cmTree2<-table(baseball_test$pitch_type, predTree2)
cmTree2

#### CORRECT RATE
mean(baseball_test$pitch_type==predTree2)
```
